dependencies: Third-party investment managers manage the majority of investment portfolio
Reliance on third-party providers for business and administrative services including policy administration, finance, actuarial, information technology services, and investment advisory services
Reliance on external distribution partners including brokers, agents, advisors, marketplaces
Dependence on reinsurance market for risk transfer
risks: Disruptions or failures by third-party providers could cause operational difficulties, inability to meet obligations, loss of business, increased costs, reputational harm, or data integrity compromises
Reinsurance may be unavailable or too expensive relative to its benefit
Third-party providers may not perform as anticipated or fail to meet licensure requirements
Supply chain disruptions or delays could prevent maintaining and implementing changes to systems and networks
Third-party providers located outside the U.S. expose to business disruptions and political risks
Credit risk with respect to reinsurers if reinsurance receivable is not secured
Market conditions may impact availability and cost of reinsurance

regulatory_risks: Climate change-related disclosure requirements and regulations
Terrorism Risk Insurance Program Reauthorization Act (TRIPRA) requirements
Changes to tax laws including corporate alternative minimum tax (CAMT) and OECD proposals
Japan Financial Services Agency extensive authority under Insurance Business Act
Credit rating downgrade triggers for collateral posting
State insurance laws provide that dividends in excess of certain prescribed limits require prior approval from applicable insurance regulator
Changes in laws and regulations may affect operations, increase insurance subsidiary capital requirements or reduce profitability
Potential for regulatory actions, fines, penalties, or restrictions for non-compliance
Clawback policy requirements for executive compensation
Heavily regulated businesses subject to extensive regulation and supervision in jurisdictions where insurance businesses are located or operate
Privacy, data protection, cybersecurity and artificial intelligence requirements
Pension plan funding requirements under ERISA
Own Risk and Solvency Assessment (ORSA) requirements
Credit Support Annex provisions for derivatives
Workers' compensation regulatory requirements by state
Heightened regulatory scrutiny on environmental, social, governance and sustainability matters

Insurance regulatory authorities conduct periodic examinations covering financial strength, sales, marketing, claims handling practices, risk management, capital and liquidity management, and information technology operations
Terrorism Risk Insurance Program Reauthorization Act (TRIPRA) compliance
Risk-Based Capital (RBC) requirements for insurance companies
Solvency II governs insurance industry solvency framework for EU operations
Potential conflicts between regulations across different jurisdictions
NAIC Data Security Model Law requirements for information security programs
Insurance contract accounting standards (GAAP vs statutory)
State regulations on use of big data, AI, machine learning in insurance marketplace
Risk-Based Capital (RBC) requirements and potential regulatory actions if RBC levels fall below specific thresholds
UK, EU, Japan and other jurisdictions maintain similar anti-corruption and sanctions laws
Privacy and data protection laws including GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, NYDFS cybersecurity regulation, EU Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA)
Section 382 ownership change limitations on use of tax loss carryforwards
Statutory accounting practices prescribed by domestic and foreign insurance regulatory authorities
Regulatory restrictions on dividend payments by insurance subsidiaries
State insurance laws restricting dividends without prior regulatory approval
Group Capital Calculation (GCC) requirement for ultimate controlling person of U.S. insurers
NAIC Insurance Holding Company System Regulatory Act requirements including enterprise risk reporting and regulatory approval for certain transactions
Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act provisions
Federal Home Loan Bank (FHLB) membership requirements
Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctions regulations

New laws and regulations or new interpretations of current laws and regulations, both domestically and internationally, may affect businesses, results of operations, financial condition and ability to compete effectively
GILTI (Global Intangible Low-Taxed Income) tax requirements
SEC climate-related disclosure rules (stayed pending judicial review)
Singapore Insurance Act 1966 regulates insurance business with minimum capital and solvency requirements
Bermuda Insurance Act 1978 imposes solvency and liquidity standards, auditing and reporting requirements
Financial Stability Oversight Council authority to designate nonbank systemically important financial institutions (SIFIs)
Title VII of Dodd-Frank derivatives regulation including regulatory reporting, mandated clearing, and margin requirements
Foreign jurisdictions regulatory restrictions on dividend payments
Internal Revenue Code restrictions on pension contributions

sanction_risks: Political risk insurance exposures in various countries
Limitations on business activities due to sanctions
Potential sanctions compliance requirements for international operations
Compliance risks with economic sanctions regulations relating to jurisdictions where businesses operate
Trade credit insurance exposures to geopolitical events
UK, EU, Japan and other jurisdictions maintain similar sanctions laws
Potential civil and criminal penalties for sanctions violations
Russia/Ukraine conflict impact on reserves and catastrophe losses
Armed conflict between Ukraine and Russia and corresponding sanctions imposed by United States and other countries
Disruption to business operations in countries exposed to geopolitical risk
Geopolitical tensions affecting foreign currency exposures
Sanctions policies and restrictive government actions in foreign jurisdictions
Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) regulations restricting dealings with certain organizations, individuals and countries
Conflict in Israel and surrounding areas
